record.bridgyOriginalText:
Here's something you can try in a couple minutes. Write a prompt and run it once. Then ask yourself what you assumed without realizing it, and what the AI echoed back. Rephrase to challenge or clarify that assumption. Run it again. That quick cycle teaches you more than rushing through ten attempts.

Better prompts come from paying attention to what the AI says, not just what you put in. One good loop beats a dozen sloppy ones. (2/3)

record.bridgyOriginalText:
AI tends to mirror what you feed it. If you keep asking the same way, with the same assumptions, it just reflects those right back. That's how bias creeps in, even without you noticing.

The fix is straightforward: stop treating AI like a single throwaway request. Treat it like a back and forth. Get a response, see what it gave you, then adjust your next prompt based on that. That short loop makes you slow down and correct course, same as the AI. (1/3)
